agent_raw: The chest X-ray prioritization model validation is methodologically sound. The team used a non-inferiority framework, which is appropriate when comparing a new tool to an established process where the goal is not to claim superiority but to show the tool is not meaningfully worse. Key strengths: (1) the protocol and non-inferiority margin were pre-specified before data collection, preventing outcome-driven decisions; (2) the power analysis formally justified the approximately 600-case sample size, so the cohort size is not arbitrary; (3) 620 cases were collected consecutively, minimizing selection bias; (4) the model was evaluated once as a frozen system on an external cohort, which is the right prospective validation design; (5) the conclusion is explicitly bounded — non-inferiority supported, not superiority, and limited to similar-hardware settings. The apparent concern about a sub-1,000 sample size is a misapplication of superiority-trial standards to a non-inferiority question. The sample was adequately powered for the stated question. This is a valid validation that supports its stated conclusion.
